visa_types.name: Qatar Embassy Attestation
visa_types.documents_required:
Personal Documents:
- Original documents to be attested
- Photocopy of original document
- Correctly filled & signed application form
- Passport biopages photocopy (for both applicant & spouse in case of Marriage Certificate)
Educational Documents:
- Original documents to be attested- Should have State HRD Attestation completed.
- Bonafide certificate attested by the SDM & Ministry of External Affairs, India
- Consolidated Marksheet/Final Year marksheet attested by the SDM & Ministry of External Affairs, India
- Verification Letter from the University
- Photocopy of original document
- Correctly filled & signed application form
- Passport biopages photocopy (for both applicant & spouse in case of Marriage Certificate)

Very Important: Educational Certificate should not be issued for a distance learning program. Only full time regular educational certificates are accepted for attestation from Qatar Embassy.

- *All Packages are inclusive of applicable taxes
- We also offer pickup service where your document will be picked up from your preferred location, processed and delivered to your address. You may refer to the logistic package with pickup and delivery (INR 1100/-).

Note: please get in touch with our contact centre team for serviceable locations

visa_types.name: Lebanon Embassy Attestation
visa_types.documents_required:
- Originals along with two xerox copies of the document to be legalized.
- Correctly filled application form
- Passport Bio Pages (First & Last page of your passport with personal details)
- Documents that require Lebanon Embassy Attestation are required to be attested from the State Authentication Ministry/Chamber of Commerce and the Ministry of External Affairs, India (mandatory steps)
- Covering letter to the embassy on company’s letter head-requesting for legalization with clearly mentioning the name of each document.
- Each document should have a clear and specific title which should match with the names mentioned on the covering letter.
- Documents to be submitted at the Lebanon Embassy should be pre-attested from the Ministry of External Affairs, India.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Embassy fee click (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1eo5iAZycZKCE13xBpfkjC/889c03707878306762a5c912c7e2d4c9/consular-fees.pdf)
VFS Service fee: INR 1770 (inclusive of taxes) 
Legalization for the Lebanon Mission shall now be under the purview of VFS Global and you may submit your applications to our dedicated Application centres for any legalization request to the Lebanon Embassy, India.
PLEASE NOTE THE FOLLOWING WHILE SUBMITTING ANY APPLICATION FOR LEGALIZATION FROM THE LEBANON EMBASSY:
- Originals along with two xerox copies of the document to be legalized.
- Covering letter to the embassy on company’s letter head-requesting for legalization with clearly mentioning the name of each document.
- Each document should have a clear and specific title which should match with the names mentioned on the covering letter.
- Documents to be submitted at the Lebanon Embassy should be pre-attested from the Ministry of External Affairs, India.
To arrange for pickup & new application submission: - +91 9873611267 (Timings – 9:30 to 13:00 & 14:00 to 16:00) Monday to Friday except holidays. Please note that queries only related to services offered on this website shall be answered via this helpline and we may be unable to assist on any other service.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Since Lebanon is not a signatory to The Hague Apostille Convention, any official document destined for this country will be legalized by the Lebanon Embassy for consular and commercial purposes. Legalizing documents for Lebanon may require additional steps before the documents can be presented to the Lebanon Embassy or Consulate office.
Legalization for the Lebanon Mission shall now be under the purview of VFS Global and you may submit your applications to our dedicated Application centres for any legalization request to the Lebanon Embassy, India.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Since Lebanon is not a signatory to The Hague Apostille Convention, any official document destined for this country will be legalized by the Lebanon Embassy for consular and commercial purposes. Legalizing documents for Lebanon may require additional steps before the documents can be presented to the Lebanon Embassy or Consulate office.
Legalization for the Lebanon Mission shall now be under the purview of VFS Global and you may submit your applications to our dedicated Application centres for any legalization request to the Lebanon Embassy, India.

## Fees

Embassy fee click (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1eo5iAZycZKCE13xBpfkjC/889c03707878306762a5c912c7e2d4c9/consular-fees.pdf)
VFS Service fee: INR 1770 (inclusive of taxes) 
Legalization for the Lebanon Mission shall now be under the purview of VFS Global and you may submit your applications to our dedicated Application centres for any legalization request to the Lebanon Embassy, India.
PLEASE NOTE THE FOLLOWING WHILE SUBMITTING ANY APPLICATION FOR LEGALIZATION FROM THE LEBANON EMBASSY:
- Originals along with two xerox copies of the document to be legalized.
- Covering letter to the embassy on company’s letter head-requesting for legalization with clearly mentioning the name of each document.
- Each document should have a clear and specific title which should match with the names mentioned on the covering letter.
- Documents to be submitted at the Lebanon Embassy should be pre-attested from the Ministry of External Affairs, India.
To arrange for pickup & new application submission: - +91 9873611267 (Timings – 9:30 to 13:00 & 14:00 to 16:00) Monday to Friday except holidays. Please note that queries only related to services offered on this website shall be answered via this helpline and we may be unable to assist on any other service.

## Documents Required
- Originals along with two xerox copies of the document to be legalized.
- Correctly filled application form
- Passport Bio Pages (First & Last page of your passport with personal details)
- Documents that require Lebanon Embassy Attestation are required to be attested from the State Authentication Ministry/Chamber of Commerce and the Ministry of External Affairs, India (mandatory steps)
- Covering letter to the embassy on company’s letter head-requesting for legalization with clearly mentioning the name of each document.
- Each document should have a clear and specific title which should match with the names mentioned on the covering letter.
- Documents to be submitted at the Lebanon Embassy should be pre-attested from the Ministry of External Affairs, India.

## Download Forms
Application form (attestations)  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6wtCErTybdY5oRDGC0U9OW/ff7dfbdd0b46faf985c83c602d977313/application-form-_attestations_.pdf)

visa_types.name: UAE Embassy Attestation
visa_types.documents_required:
- Original documents to be attested
- Correctly filled application form
- Passport Bio Pages (First & Last page of your passport with personal details)
- Documents that require UAE Embassy Attestation are required to be attested from the State Authentication Ministry and the Ministry of External Affairs (mandatory steps)
- For Marriage Certificates: Passport copies of both Husband and Wife are required to be presented on the same page. (Marriage Certificates without Husband and Wife passport copies on the same page will not be accepted for UAE Attestations)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Since Vietnam is not a signatory to The Hague Apostille Convention, any official document destined for this country will be legalized by their respective Embassy/Consulate for consular and commercial purposes. Legalizing documents for Vietnam may require additional steps before the documents can be presented to the mission:

[Table:   Vietnam Embassy Attestation]
Legalization for Vietnam can now be applied through any of the VFS Centres offering Vietnam Visa Application.
Please Note: All normal/educational documents to be attested from the Vietnam Embassy are mandatorily translated into Vietnamese Language by the mission. Commercial documents do not require translation as per guidelines from the Embassy

## Documents Required
- Original document to be attested
- Correctly filled application form
- Passport Bio Pages (First & Last page of your passport with personal details)

## Download Forms
Application form (attestations)  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6wtCErTybdY5oRDGC0U9OW/ff7dfbdd0b46faf985c83c602d977313/application-form-_attestations_.pdf)

visa_types.name: Police Clearance Certificate (PCC) Attestation/Apostille
visa_types.documents_required:
- Original documents to be attested
- Photocopy of original document
- Correctly filled & signed application form
- Applicant's Passport Copy bio pages
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Police Clearance Certificate (PCC) Attestation/Apostille]
Note:
- Applicants applying for Apostille need to pay INR 50 additional to VFS Global service charges as MEA fee
- The above service fee is inclusive of taxes
- Scanning fee: INR 20 (per page)
- Temp. Update: The Ministry of External Affairs is working on limited schedule & working days due to the ongoing COVID-19 outbreak & the nationwide lockdown imposed. Hence our processing time for Apostille from the Ministry of External Affairs shall be 5-7 working days.
Note:
- *All Packages are inclusive of applicable taxes
- We also offer pickup service where your document will be picked up from your preferred location, processed and delivered to your address. You may refer to the logistic package with pickup and delivery (INR 1100/-).

Note: please get in touch with our contact centre team for serviceable locations
